title: Solubility Sorter — Dissolves in Water?
instructions_for_student: Drag each liquid or gas into one of the two bins. Read the reason on every correct drop. Tip: You can also tap an item to select it, then tap a bin to place it!

additionalProps.finalCardMessage: The same solubility rule covers solids, liquids, and gases! Oxygen dissolves in water so fish can breathe; oils do not — that is why they float. CO₂ dissolves under pressure to make soda fizzy. Solubility is a universal property of matter!

tiles.label: Lemon Juice
tiles.reason: Lemon juice mixes completely with water — it's mostly water itself!
tiles.hint: Think about what lemonade looks like in a glass…

tiles.label: Vinegar
tiles.reason: Vinegar dissolves in water — it's a dilute acid that mixes fully.
tiles.hint: Vinegar is used in salad dressings with water-based ingredients…

tiles.label: Mustard Oil
tiles.reason: Mustard oil does NOT dissolve — oil and water don't mix, forming separate layers.
tiles.hint: Have you seen oil floating on water in a cooking pan?

tiles.label: Coconut Oil
tiles.reason: Coconut oil is insoluble — all oils are hydrophobic (water-fearing).
tiles.hint: All cooking oils behave the same way with water…

tiles.label: Honey
tiles.reason: Honey dissolves in water — it's mostly sugar and water, so it mixes fully.
tiles.hint: Think about warm honey tea — the honey disappears into the water!

tiles.label: Air Bubble
tiles.reason: Air (mostly nitrogen) does NOT dissolve well — bubbles rise and escape water.
tiles.hint: Watch what happens when you blow air through a straw into water…

tiles.label: Oxygen Gas
tiles.reason: Oxygen dissolves in water! Fish breathe this dissolved oxygen to survive.
tiles.hint: How do fish breathe underwater if there's no air?

tiles.label: CO₂ in Soda
tiles.reason: CO₂ dissolves in water under pressure — that's what makes soda fizzy!
tiles.hint: What happens when you open a soda bottle?

agent_readme.description: A two-bin drag-and-drop classifier for teaching solubility of liquids and gases in water. Eight tiles appear; students sort them into 'Dissolves in Water' or 'Does NOT Dissolve'. Correct drops lock in green with a reason; wrong drops shake and return with a hint. Completion shows a summary card reinforcing that the rule applies across states of matter.
agent_readme.curriculum_link: NCERT Grade 6 Science — Chapter 6.3.4: What is soluble in water; what is not?

tiles.description: Array of tile objects. Defaults to the 8 canonical items if omitted.

finalCardMessage.description: Text shown on the completion card. Defaults to a cross-state-of-matter summary.

interaction_modes.drag_and_drop: Desktop — drag a tile onto a bin header area
interaction_modes.tap_to_select_then_tap_bin: Mobile / touch — tap a tile (highlighted in orange), then tap the target bin

scoring.score_strip: Live score fraction + attempt counter + progress bar
scoring.correct_drop: Tile locks with green background in the bin
scoring.wrong_drop: Tile shakes, returns, hint revealed, counter incremented
scoring.completion: Full-screen overlay with score, rule summary, and Try Again button

default_8_tiles.description: Omit additionalProps.tiles to use the built-in set

solids_only_quiz.description: Override tiles to test solids instead

tiles.label: Salt
tiles.reason: Salt dissolves fully — that's why seawater is salty.
tiles.hint: Stir salt into water and watch it disappear…

tiles.label: Sand
tiles.reason: Sand is insoluble — it sinks to the bottom.

tiles.label: Chalk Powder
tiles.reason: Chalk does not dissolve — it stays suspended or sinks.
tiles.hint: Mix chalk in water and wait a few minutes…

tiles.label: Sawdust
tiles.reason: Sawdust is insoluble and floats on water.
tiles.hint: Wood doesn't disappear when it gets wet…

additionalProps.finalCardMessage: Great start! Now try the full 8-tile challenge.

tiles.label: Honey
tiles.hint: Think about honey in hot tea…

decision_tree.student_asks_about_oils_vs_juices: Use default tiles; emphasise the mustard/coconut oil tiles
decision_tree.student_asks_about_solids: Override tiles with solid examples (see solids_only_quiz example)
decision_tree.student_asks_about_gases_in_water: Highlight oxygen and CO₂ tiles in the default set
decision_tree.full_chapter_6_review: Use default 8 tiles with default finalCardMessage
